perturbation.original_text: DEFINITIONS:All information, concerning the business of the other Party, hereafter in the possession of either of theParties, or their respective directors, officers, employees, agents or representatives, including, withoutlimitation, lawyers, accountants, consultants and financial advisors (collectively, "Representatives") andexcept as noted hereinafter is considered to be "Confidential Information".
perturbation.changed_text: DEFINITIONS:All information, concerning the business of the other Party, hereafter in the possession of either of theParties, or their respective directors, officers, employees, agents or representatives, including, withoutlimitation, lawyers, accountants, consultants and financial advisors (collectively, "Representatives") is considered to be "Confidential Information".
perturbation.explanation: By removing "and except as noted hereinafter", all information is considered confidential, regardless of the exceptions listed later in the agreement. This contradicts the section that follows which explicitly excludes certain information from being considered Confidential Information, such as information already in the public domain. This creates uncertainty about what information is actually protected under the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The following information is excluded from being considered Confidential Information:(i) Information which, at the time of disclosure, is in the public domain, or information whichsubsequently becomes part of the public domain, by means other than breach of this Agreement.(ii) Information which the receiving Party obtains from a third party having a lawful right to disclosesaid information(iii) Information which can be shown by competent written evidence to have been developed by thereceiving Party independently of said disclosure.(iv) Information which is required to be disclosed to any third party by reason of an applicable law, orby the decision of any court or tribunal having competent jurisdiction, and provided that written andreasonable prior notice has been given to the disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text includes 'and except as noted hereinafter,' which allows for exceptions to the definition of 'Confidential Information.' The changed text removes this phrase, meaning that all information concerning the business of the other party is considered 'Confidential Information,' regardless of any exceptions. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which explicitly lists categories of information excluded from being considered 'Confidential Information.' Therefore, removing this phrase creates a direct contradiction within the document, as defined in the explanation and adhering to the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' type.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties will not, and will direct their respective representatives and associates not to, disclose toany other person that the Confidential Information has been made available to them, that discussions ornegotiations are taking place concerning a potential Transaction or any of the terms, conditions or otherfacts with respect to any such Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties will, at their sole discretion, decide whether or not direct their respective representatives and associates to disclose to any other person that the Confidential Information has been made available to them, that discussions ornegotiations are taking place concerning a potential Transaction or any of the terms, conditions or otherfacts with respect to any such Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: By adding "at their sole discretion, decide whether or not" the obligation to not disclose Confidential Information is now optional. This directly contradicts the general spirit and purpose of a non-disclosure agreement and creates conflict, it weakens the agreement and makes it unsure if the parties should disclose this type of information or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties will (and shall ensure that their respective Representatives will) keep the ConfidentialInformation confidential and will not, without the prior written consent of the other Party, disclose anyConfidential Information in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part, and will not use ConfidentialInformation, directly or indirectly, for other than evaluating a possible Transaction. The Parties shall Reciprocal Confidential Non-Disclosure Agreement 3.12 of 3make all reasonable, necessary or appropriate efforts to safeguard the Confidential Information fromdisclosure to anyone other than as permitted hereby.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces "at their sole discretion, decide whether or not direct" which makes the disclosure of Confidential Information optional. The contradicted text explicitly states that "The Parties will (and shall ensure that their respective Representatives will) keep the ConfidentialInformation confidential and will not...disclose any Confidential Information". Therefore, the changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text by making the confidentiality obligation optional, where the contradicted text mandates it.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties will (and shall ensure that their respective Representatives will) keep the ConfidentialInformation confidential and will not, without the prior written consent of the other Party, disclose anyConfidential Information in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part, and will not use ConfidentialInformation, directly or indirectly, for other than evaluating a possible Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties will (and shall ensure that their respective Representatives will) strive to keep the ConfidentialInformation confidential and will not, without the prior written consent of the other Party, disclose anyConfidential Information in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part, and will not use ConfidentialInformation, directly or indirectly, for other than evaluating a possible Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "keep" to "strive to keep" weakens the obligation to maintain confidentiality. This creates an in-text contradiction with the clause specifying injunctive relief in Section 5, which implies a strong, enforceable obligation to maintain confidentiality. Striving to keep something confidential introduces ambiguity and reduces the likelihood of injunctive relief being granted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party acknowledges and agrees that the other Party would not have an adequate remedy at lawand would be irreparably harmed if any of the provisions of this Agreement were not performed inaccordance with their specific terms or were otherwise breached. Accordingly, the Parties agree thateither Party shall be entitled to injunctive relief to prevent breaches of this Agreement and to specificallyenforce the terms and provisions hereof in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled at lawor in equity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the obligation to maintain confidentiality from "will keep" to "strive to keep." This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the parties would be irreparably harmed if the agreement's provisions were not performed in accordance with their specific terms and that either party shall be entitled to injunctive relief. "Strive to keep" introduces ambiguity and weakens the enforceability of the confidentiality clause, making injunctive relief less likely and directly conflicting with the intent of the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Information or concepts which are discussed by either Party as an extrapolation of the ConfidentialInformation disclosed are the property of the topic's disclosing Party. For greater clarity, the discussionor invention of an extension to Confidential Information does not give the uttering Party intellectualproperty rights to that aspect of the topic unless the related Confidential Information is theirs.
perturbation.changed_text: Information or concepts which are discussed by either Party as an extrapolation of the ConfidentialInformation disclosed are the property of both parties. For greater clarity, the discussionor invention of an extension to Confidential Information does give the uttering Party intellectualproperty rights to that aspect of the topic even if the related Confidential Information is theirs.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "property of the topic's disclosing Party" to "property of both parties" and also changing the condition on the "intellectual property rights" creates a direct contradiction. The original states clearly that extrapolation of confidential information belongs to the disclosing party and no property rights are being passed. The modification now states the opposite: that is shared and rights are passed. This contradicts the initial agreement that protects only the disclosing party
perturbation.contradicted_text: Information or concepts which are discussed by either Party as an extrapolation of the ConfidentialInformation disclosed are the property of both parties. For greater clarity, the discussionor invention of an extension to Confidential Information does give the uttering Party intellectualproperty rights to that aspect of the topic even if the related Confidential Information is theirs.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original intent regarding intellectual property rights of extrapolated confidential information. The original text assigns ownership to the disclosing party, while the changed text assigns ownership to both parties and grants intellectual property rights to the uttering party, even if the confidential information isn't theirs. This creates a clear conflict with the original agreement, and with the contradicted text since they are both the same. The explanation details that the initial agreement only protects the disclosing party.
